TextENGNew: I could tell Otobe had just drawn a sharp breath.
A thin line of bright red blood trailed from Towada-san's mouth — as if declaring that she had already passed.

Miyuki-chan rushed to Towada-san's side and pressed her ear close to her mouth. After a slight grimace, she placed her hand on Towada-san's neck while checking her wristwatch... then shook her head in defeat.
"It's no use... she's gone."
"--Don't touch that!"
changeReason: Scope narrowed: 'Don't touch anything!' to 'Don't touch that!' (JPN 触らないで is a general 'don't touch' but in context refers specifically to the teacup).

TextENGNew: I reached for the fallen teacup, but Miyuki-chan stopped me with a sharp command. She pulled a pouch from her knapsack and unzipped it.
As I stood there bewildered, she pulled out some kind of paper strip and dipped it in the teacup. The paper's color began to change before my eyes.
I could vaguely tell it was some kind of chemical test strip, but... being completely ignorant in that field, I had no idea what substance it was detecting.
And more to the point — why did she have something like that in the first place...?
"...I thought so.@k@vS10/40/410300807. But to think she was carrying something like this on her..."
Miyuki-chan said that with a bitter expression and set the teacup down.
"...Good grief.@k@vS10/43/410400500. There are more corpses in here?"
"...Don't think that this is your fault.@k@vS10/43/410400502. This is what that girl chose to do."
Sonozaki-san glanced at Towada-san's corpse for a moment, then walked past us and started washing her blood and mud-caked hands in the kitchen.
Then, Miyuki-chan pulled out a camera with a disheartened expression on her face and started photographing the state of the body.
She didn't waste a moment... Her craftsmanship was as precise as an experienced police investigator.
Wait, that's not what matters at the moment...!
"W-wait...@k@vS10/62/410101387. Please, explain what's going on. I want to understand!"

"...Unfortunately, we have no way of contacting emergency responders, so any attempt at first aid would be pointless.@k@vS10/40/410300810. I was able to detect traces of poison in this teacup.@k@vS10/40/410300811. And what's worse, this is a powerful, fast-acting drug... so it's already too late to help her."
"Does that mean... Towada-san... committed suicide?!"

"When we went out to fight those men dressed in black, she alone stayed here.@k@vS10/40/410300813. She... probably intended to die if her plan fell apart...@k@vS10/40/410300814. Or maybe this was her plan from the moment she chose to come to this village with him..."
I didn't know what to say.
We were all in serious danger until just a moment ago. We were on the brink of death.
But... it wasn't like that for Towada-san. She personally chose to die.
If I was able to hear Towada-san's story to the end, maybe I could've changed her mind.
Maybe I could've done something.
Or maybe... everything was already beyond my ability to help, dictated by some immutable fate...
Could it be... that she felt some responsibility that led her to carry out murder...?
If she did, I hadn't heard any of it.
What led Towada-san to murder? There must have been some deep-seated motivation for it... but she never gave us an opportunity to hear it... before dying, herself.
I knew the fact that she killed someone and the consequences of it, but was that really everything...? Was taking her own life the only way to atone for it...?
Is human life... really such a mechanical concept...?
...Coarse feelings swirled around inside my chest, far beyond simple words like regret or sorrow.
"Suicide... huh...?"
Otobe muttered that to himself.
Towada-san and Kurosawa looked like they were sleeping huddled together.
It looked like a beautiful double suicide, a fitting end for an impossible love... but is there really beauty in something like that...?
A beautiful suicide...? There's no such thing...!
"How long are you all going to aimlessly laze about?@k@vS10/43/410400504. ...They're dead. You could stare at them the rest of your lives and they'll never wake up."
Sonozaki-san's words brought me back to my senses.
That was certainly true. ...There was nothing we could do for her at this point, other than pray.
And there was still another threat that placed our own lives in jeopardy...
"Anyway, get some sleep while you have the chance.@k@vS10/43/410400506. They won't come back tonight.@k@vS10/43/410400507. ...Probably."
"...Okay."
Sonozaki-san sat down on the tatami with her back against a pillar and closed her eyes, sword still in hand.
Otobe and I followed her lead and took a seat on the floor.
Miyuki-chan sat down beside me and started performing maintenance on her freshly used gun.
"M-Miyuki-ch..."
"Ryuunosuke-san.@k@vS10/40/410300816. ...Don't you think it would be best to lie down now and get some sleep?"
"Ah... right..."
Miyuki-chan... seriously... who the hell are you...?
I stole a quick glance at her face.
I was convinced that even if I asked, she wouldn't tell me the truth... but I still wanted to know.
"Please get some sleep.@k@vS10/40/410300818. Don't worry about me."
"...Good night, Ryuunosuke-san."
"Good night..."
Though she spoke in a discouraging voice, she gently smiled as she said it.
...In the end, I couldn't ask her anything, so I simply laid down on the tatami and closed my eyes.
"Arakawa-san... are you going to sleep...?"
"Yeah, I'm trying to get some sleep."
"Umm... in that case, shouldn't we turn out the lights...?"
"I don't mind either way.@k@vS10/62/410101397. ...If you want them off, you can take care of it yourself."
"Ah, no.@k@vS10/40/410300821. I think it would be best to leave the lights on."
Miyuki-chan remained seated as she said that to Otobe. ...It was less a suggestion and more an order.
"Just in case of an emergency.@k@vS10/40/410300823. If you close your eyes, it's pretty much the same."
"Right..."
"You too, Otobe-kun. Get some sleep.@k@vS10/40/410300825. ...I'm going to bed too."
Her voice was excessively gentle. Like a mother addressing her child.
Miyuki-chan laid down right beside me.
"Good night..."
The smell of Miyuki-chan's hair wafted over to my nose.
Just last night, I would've been thrilled to have Miyuki-chan sleeping next to me. But now...
Her hair smelled fragrant and attractive before we came to this village... but now, it smelled like gunpowder and blood.
I felt slightly disappointed as I watched Miyuki-chan close her eyes.
If only she were just a freelance writer...
If only she were a normal girl...
Then we could've gone on a nice drive together this weekend...
"Good night."
Otobe finally laid down.
The three of us were lying straight in a line next to each other.
The sound of rustling clothing awakened me.
The sky was clear and sunny outside the window.
The birds who had just awoken began to sing.
Otobe was still sleeping right in front of me.
But when I turned and looked in the other direction... nobody was there.
I rose to my feet in a hurry.
Sonozaki-san was walking toward the doorway.
"Sonozaki-san..."
"...You should all stay here and sleep."
"W-where are you going?!"
Otobe must've heard my voice, and started to wake up.
Kurosawa and Towada-san were still lying dead in front of the closet like they were sleeping. It wasn't much different from last night. But...
"W-where's Miyuki-chan...?"
I looked all over the assembly room, but Miyuki-chan was nowhere to be found. And I had a feeling she wasn't in the bathroom.
"That girl left before the sun came up."
"She left...? Where did she go?!"
"Where? I have no idea."
"That can't be true?!@k@vS10/62/410101405. You know something, don't you?!"
"I don't know.@k@vS10/43/410400512. I've been focused on finishing my business."
After saying that, Sonozaki-san tried to leave the meeting room.
"H... hold on!@k@vS10/62/410101407. ...Where do you think you're going?!"
"To take care of a little business at home."
"At home...?"
"Isn't it obvious?@k@vS10/43/410400515. I'm going to the main Sonozaki house."
"You... what are you planning to do at a place like that...?"
"Those men in black who came here last night... they were coming for you, weren't they...?"
"Let's set that conversation aside for now.@k@vS10/43/410400518. ...Haven't you heard the saying, curiosity killed the cat?"
Sonozaki-san said only that, then quickly stepped out of the meeting room.

"H-h-hold on?@k@vS10/62/410101411. You can't leave me here with just that!@k@vS10/62/410101412. Besides, it's not about curiosity. I'm already involved in this, so aren't we all in the same boat here?!"
"That goes for me too...!@k@vS10/41/410200712. I can't stand being left alone in a place like this!"
"...Then it can't be helped.@k@vS10/43/410400520. I won't wait long, so prepare quickly."
I jumped up and grabbed my luggage, then chased after Sonozaki-san. When Otobe saw that, he came running over in a hurry.
The rain had completely stopped.
The fresh morning air surrounded my entire body.
Seeing the shrine gate under the sun, it looked far less majestic than last night. It was just a small old-fashioned shrine gate.
Sonozaki-san quickly descended the stone steps, then began walking down the country road.
"...Which way are we headed?"
"I wonder...?"
It seemed like Sonozaki-san wasn't interested in answering.
"Let's take a shortcut."
After a while, Sonozaki-san stopped at the middle of a grove of trees like it was some sort of landmark, then advanced through the natural trail between them.
"Wha...?!@k@vS10/62/410101415. A-are you seriously going through a place like this?"
"If you've got a problem, you guys can go take the normal road."
"...Understood!"
"Ah, please wait..."
Otobe followed closely behind Sonozaki-san as she walked down the trail. Naturally, I started to follow a little afterwards.
"H-hey, Otobe, wait up...!"
...Honestly, I was a bit anxious about being the back of the line. I could picture the unreliable companions in front of me leaving me behind.
(...Seriously!)
I've come this far, I won't get left behind now!
I pumped my legs to shorten the distance between us.
The rain or morning dew on the wet grass rubbed against the hem of my pants. ...A tree frog leaped from a lush, green leaf.
"This is..."
Before long, we arrived back on a country road.
A vivid white cloud was floating just over the horizon, and a variety of lush vegetation was spread out before us. The road continued forward with old houses along both sides.
"...It's a village."
"Are you talking in your sleep?@k@vS10/43/410400525. It's the same village we could see from the shrine... This is Hinamizawa."
Sonozaki-san continued swiftly walking as she said that.
Spider webs were stretched across a decayed wooden house. The windows were broken and glass was scattered all around.
"...Haah.@k@vS10/62/410101421. I didn't really notice when I was looking at the mountains and the trees, but... when I look at this house it really does look like an abandoned village."
Suddenly, I remembered that I came here for work... But I was in no mood to be taking pictures.
After everything that's happened here, I couldn't bring myself to think about journalism.
"Something wrong?"
"Oh, no.@k@vS10/41/410200718. I was just thinking that even though these houses are really old, there's one that doesn't look so old..."
Otobe stopped in his tracks, right in front of a huge white mansion.
"Whoa, now that's a humongous house."
"I feel like someone rich must've lived there."
Sonozaki-san stopped beside us, then gently gazed at the mansion.
It seemed like she had completely tuned out our existence... like she was remembering something.
Her eyes looked gentle... and she had a bit of a smile on her face... with a hint of sadness mixed in with it...
"...chan."
"H-hey! What's wrong?@k@vS10/62/410101425. You stopped all of a sudden..."
"Pardon me for stopping.@k@vS10/43/410400530. Let's go."
Sonozaki-san gazed at the mansion for a while with a nostalgic look in her eyes, but before long she wiped the corner of her eye and started walking again.
Sonozaki-san said she was a resident of this village.
If that's the truth... then maybe she has some precious memories of the person who lived in that house...?
"...Well, whatever."
Honestly, I was interested to learn more... but this wasn't the time.
Sonozaki-san was walking at such a brisk pace I had a hard enough time keeping up with her, let alone time to think idle thoughts.
Eventually, we arrived at a huge estate surrounded by a large fence.
The size and construction were completely different from the other houses. It had a style befitting the Three Great Families.

And the front gate had several retro-style security cameras that looked like toys. Of course, they've been neglected and left exposed to the rain for over 20 years, so there was no way they could still work...
The entire house had a different sort of atmosphere to it.
The high fences, security cameras, and ornate double doors illustrated how strongly protected the Sonozaki family was.
I focused my attention on my surroundings... and then, I froze.
Hiding behind the shadows of the door... were the men in black from last night, waiting for us.
In full sunlight, I could clearly make out the faces of the men.
No matter how I looked at them... they were all obviously yakuza.
"What...? They're here too...?"
"What are you saying? This is all to be expected.@k@vS10/43/410400532. ...Now, we have no choice but to handle it."
Sonozaki-san grabbed her katana. And as if on cue, the men in black each stood up and grabbed their weapons.
"H-hold on?@k@vS10/62/410101431. Handle it...? We're unarmed!@k@vS10/62/410101432. Besides, I don't want to get involved in some gangster conflict!"
"I understand that.@k@vS10/43/410400534. But there's a hidden passage in this estate that leads out of the village.@k@vS10/43/410400535. You guys will be able to escape through there!"
A gunshot rang out before Sonozaki-san could finish speaking.
But she already started running. In the direction it came from... straight ahead!
It felt like Sonozaki-san was trying to break through the front gate...!
"Huh? Are you serious?!@k@vS10/62/410101434. Damn it!!"
"A-Arakawa-san?!"
Otobe and I followed Sonozaki-san's lead and started running.
"Don't let them escape! She's a monster!"
"... A monster?"
*bang*
In the brief moment I was distracted by the voice of the man in black, a bullet flew by and hit the gate of the Sonozaki residence right behind my back.
"Aah!"
"Eek!"
It seemed like they were trying to repel our advance. Two more dry gunshots roared out.
"These guys... are trying to kill us too?!"
"Of course.@k@vS10/43/410400538. It's not so odd for them to think you're my allies!"
"But, why...?"
"Arakawa...!"
Sonozaki-san lifted her hand in front of my body and struck down on my head.
I tumbled forward and fell to the ground.
Immediately after that, a bullet pierced through Sonozaki-san's sleeve.
"Eeeek?!"
"There's no time for your grumbling!@k@vS10/43/410400541. If you want to survive, hurry up and go!"
And then, Sonozaki-san pushed on my back to thrust me forward.
The force of that thrust pushed my entire body toward the gate. I rolled on the ground a bit as I passed through the gate.
"Hurry, get inside...!@k@vS10/43/410400543. The hidden passage is beyond the well!"
"Arakawa-san, this way!"
"Damn iiit!"
Otobe had entered through the side gate, and I ran in the direction he beckoned.
I ran and ran like crazy.
...Meanwhile, Sonozaki-san was fighting the men behind us with her katana, doing her best to protect us.
She struck one in the top of the head with the back of her sword, then sliced upward through the pit of his stomach... The men in black started collapsing, one after another.
"You too, Sonozaki-san. Hurry...!"
"I'll catch up later.@k@vS10/43/410400545. Once you cross the garden, you'll find a forest.@k@vS10/43/410400546. Head there, and keep running!"
I couldn't even spare a moment to look back at Sonozaki-san.
The enemies were constantly attacking, and she was fighting them off alone.
"Sonozaki-san...!"
"Arakawa-san!@k@vS10/41/410200725. We need to get moving!"
"G-got it!!"
Otobe and I took off running at full speed.
Eventually, we reached a desolate forest beyond the garden, just as Sonozaki-san said.
"This forest is part of the Sonozaki estate...?@k@vS10/62/410101446. I can't believe it."
"Should we go into the forest?"
"Do you not want to go...?"
Though I was still confused... I kept running.
I couldn't see the men in black.
I wonder if Sonozaki-san is still fighting at the front gate of the estate...?
As I followed the trail through the forest... I came across a tunnel that looked like a coal mine or a shelter in the side of a steep slope.
Surrounded by gentle slopes, this spot was impossible to see from the outside.
"Could this be it...? The secret passage?!"
"Right here...?!"
A sturdy door was installed here, likely to intimidate any who would come near.
...What's this?
Something felt odd.
When I studied the door in front of me, something felt out of place.
As I quietly gazed at it... my breathing became rough and erratic.
The door... was slightly open.
"...What's wrong?"
"...The door... is open."
"The village was abandoned. Wouldn't it make sense for it to be open?"
"Ah, ...hey."
Otobe nonchalantly approached the door.
I started to move toward the passageway too... but then stopped in my tracks. A rotten, bloody stench struck my nose.
"Are we sure this is it?@k@vS10/62/410101453. The secret passage she told us about...?"
"But... if we don't go in... then we'll just get killed by the men in black out there, right?"
"That's true, but...@k@vS10/62/410101455. yeah, you're right!"
It couldn't be helped...
I took a moment to prepare myself, then vigorously pulled the handle.
Both the double doors came open... and a fishy odor burst out along with a swarm of small insects.
It was pitch black inside, so I couldn't see very well.
"Is this really it...?"
"Weren't you the one who said this was the only way?"
"That's true, but... after all...@k@vS10/41/410200733. doesn't it look really dangerous...?"
"For now, let's head a little farther inside..."
We entered the wood-lined tunnel together.
This may have been used as shelter or a storage facility during wartime.
"Mion-san said she would catch up with us later, but...@k@vS10/41/410200735. do you think she's really coming?"
"How would I know that...?"
"I'm sure she'll come!@k@vS10/62/410101460. Don't worry."
"...Okay."
I was partially saying that to myself, as well.
I completely understood Otobe's unease... because I felt the same way.
I stumbled forward through the dark passage with a hand on the wall.
"Mion-san... Will she be all right on her own?"
"I dunno, but... even if we stayed behind with her, wouldn't we just get in her way?"
"That's true, but...@k@vS10/41/410200740. it doesn't really feel like the right thing to do.@k@vS10/41/410200741. We ran away and left her back there..."
I clicked my tongue.
Otobe's insecurity was quickly pushing the limits of my patience.
"If you've got any more complaints, you're welcome to go back!"
When I raised my voice it became hard to breathe.
The oxygen levels might be lower in this secret passageway...
...No, that's not it.
It's not that the air is thinner.
My whole body felt heavy.
"...?!@k@vS10/41/410200743. Arakawa-san...!@k@vS10/41/410200744. Did you hear a sound just now?!"
"A sound...?@k@vS10/62/410101465. There shouldn't be anybody else here!"
"But... I'm sure I heard it..."
Otobe quickly fell to his knees as he said that.
"Otobe...?"
"S-sorry...@k@vS10/41/410200747. It's painful...@k@vS10/41/410200748. to stand... I can't do it..."
"Hey, Otobe...!"
What is going on...?
I couldn't see anything in the dark. What should I do...? What can I do about this...?
...That's right.
If I use my camera's flash, I should be able to see my surroundings for at least a moment.
I reached into my pocket to pull out my camera... but my hand began to tremble and I couldn't get a grip on it...!
What is the meaning of this...?!
My hands were trembling beyond my ability to control.
Otobe. I wanted to say his name, but I couldn't speak clearly.
Somehow, I managed to push the shutter with my trembling hands.
*click*
For a brief moment, the surrounding area was bathed in light.
And in that moment... I saw insects clinging to the walls, and a trail of white smoke running along the floor of the passageway.
Is this smoke... poison gas...?!
By the time I noticed it, it was already too late.
One of the men in black must've ran ahead here and filled this area with poison gas.
That's right. I wish I had been more cautious when I found the door open...
I don't have any time left for regrets.
The way I live may already be decided, but the way I die is not.
That much is certainly true... I absolutely don't want... to die quietly in a filthy, dark, smelly place where nobody will find me.
I don't want to die...
I'm sure nobody will ever even know that I died here.
My family won't know where to look for me.
Even if Otobe's family gets worried and they issue a search request... they'll never find us in here...
Aah, I don't want to die... Not like this... I never wanted this...
But my regrets are in vain, because my thoughts will cease soon enough...
Damn it... Damn iiit...!!
